content.text:
Module:
Modelling and Simulation of Networks  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEM1
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
0  |
TP:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Introduce students to the problems of modelling and performance evaluation of computer systems and communication networks
Provide methods and tools essential for the dimensioning of any network architecture or protocol
Teach discrete-event simulation techniques as design and performance analysis aids
Students will practice simulation software used in academic and industrial environments such as NS2 and OMNET++
Recommended Prerequisites
Basic concepts of computer networks
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Performance issues related to computer systems and networks
Analytical performance modelling methods (queuing theory, Petri Nets, stochastic processes)
Simulation tools (discrete-event simulations)
Simulation software, e.g., NS2, OMNET++
Random number generation
Simulation data analysis
Case studies: cache performance; traffic engineering concepts; bandwidth management; modelling and impact of traffic on network performance
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
M. A. Niazi, "Modeling and Simulation of Complex Communication Networks", Institution of Engineering and Technology, 2019.
K. Chen, "Évaluation de performances par simulation et analyse - applications aux réseaux informatiques", Editions iSTE, 2014.
R. M. Fujimoto, G. Riley et K. Perumalla, "Network Simulation", Morgan and Claypool Publishers, 2006.
